summary: The presented case shows five modifiable predictors linked to a fear-activated response to social evaluation: intrusive visual replay (C01), persistent rumination (C02), acute physiological reactivity to evaluative cues (C03), anticipatory fear of humiliation (C04), and heightened sensitivity to criticism (C05). The proposed levers span BIO/PSYCHO/SOCIAL domains and their interactions, with a focus on: (S01) imagery-based interventions to reduce involuntary re-experiencing; (S02) cognitive-behavioral strategies to curb rumination; (S03) physiological regulation to dampen autonomic arousal; (S04) graded social-evaluative exposure with cognitive reframing; and (S05) cognitive reappraisal of feedback cues. Each lever is a changeable predictor, mapped to its target criterion, with practical measurement, a feasible time horizon, and expected impact to guide implementation and evaluation.

solutions.predictor_variable: Intrusive visual replay - Imagery-based modulation (IRT/imagery rescripting)
solutions.solution_candidate: Imagery Rescripting and Imagery-based Exposure (IRT/IE) to rewrite the intrusive clip, with brief physiological grounding (breathing/grounding) before/after recall.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces involuntary sensory re-experiencing by updating memory representations and strengthening non-threat associations through dual-representation and inhibitory learning; lowers perceptual vividness and emotional intensity of the replay.
solutions.measurement: Daily intrusion diary (frequency and vividness 0-10); brief post-recall rating of emotional intensity (0-100 VAS).
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor distress during memory recall; provide optional guidance and crisis resources if distress escalates.
solutions.mapping_query: CASE: Address C01 via Imagery Rescripting/Imagery Exposure; track intrusion frequency/vividness; adjust exposure plan based on response.

solutions.solution_candidate: CBT-based rumination reduction: scheduled worry time, attention training, cognitive defusion techniques, and activity scheduling to reduce dwelling on the event.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Decreases cognitive load from perseverative thinking, disrupts negative reinforcement cycles, and improves cognitive control over intrusive narratives.
solutions.measurement: Ruminative Thought Questionnaire items; daily time spent ruminating (mins); diary-based worry episodes (0/1) per day.
solutions.mapping_query: CASE: Apply CBT-based rumination reduction; monitor changes in C02; adjust interventions per response.

solutions.solution_candidate: Physiological regulation during evaluative moments: paced breathing (4-6 breaths/min), diaphragmatic breathing, brief HRV biofeedback, and progressive muscle relaxation; include interoceptive exposure to evaluative cues.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Dampens autonomic arousal and interoceptive sensitivity to evaluative cues, increasing tolerance to feedback and reducing stomach-dropping sensations.
solutions.measurement: Self-report distress during feedback (0-10) and HRV metrics during feedback sessions; frequency of autonomic episodes per week.
solutions.safety_notes: Breathing exercises should be paced to avoid dizziness; discontinue if vertigo or chest discomfort occurs.
solutions.mapping_query: CASE: Implement physiological regulation during evaluative moments; monitor C03 responses; adjust intensity.

solutions.solution_candidate: Graded social-evaluative exposure with cognitive reframing: create a hierarchy of evaluative scenarios, combine exposure with self-compassion and mindfulness strategies, and reinforce successful encounters.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Exposure-based learning reduces threat expectancy; cognitive reframing mitigates catastrophic predictions and shame sensitivity.
solutions.measurement: Pre- and post-exposure anticipatory fear ratings (0-10); number of avoided scenarios; task completion rate from hierarchy.
solutions.safety_notes: If avoidance leads to significant impairment, pair with therapist-guided exposure or consider safety planning.
solutions.mapping_query: CASE: Address C04 with graded social-evaluative exposure plus cognitive reframing; track anticipatory fear and avoidance.

solutions.solution_candidate: Cognitive reappraisal and interpretation bias modification for feedback cues: train flexible re-interpretation of neutral/minor critique as non-threatening; incorporate self-compassion and corrective feedback logs.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces automatic threat appraisals, enhances emotional regulation during feedback, and broadens tolerance to everyday critique.
solutions.measurement: Feedback interpretation task scores (initial vs. reinterpreted); daily self-reported threat level to feedback (0-10); frequency of positive feedback acknowledgment.
solutions.mapping_query: CASE: Apply cognitive reappraisal to feedback cues; monitor C05 changes; adjust strategies as needed.
